anchored: Ed25519 primitive (pubkey + signature) vs RFC 8032 §7.1 TEST 1/TEST 2
signing_input + signature vs an independent crypto/ed25519 (oracle leg) — guards the vector
certverify_value vs SignCertVerify + certVerifySigningInput; VerifyCertVerify accept + role-mismatch reject + wrong-transcript reject (impl leg) — guards the impl
not_covered_here: transcript CONSTRUCTION (transcript KAT) and finished_key/Finished (key-schedule + Finished KATs)
ML-DSA / High / Sovereign signatures: this vector is Standard/Ed25519

name: N-PAMP CertVerify KAT

npamp_inputs.description: Keys = RFC 8032 test keypairs; th_sid/th_cid = Transcript KAT points (transcript-kat.json) the CertVerify signs per spec §6.1.

provenance: Standards-derived, non-circular. CertVerify value = u16(0x0807) || Ed25519(priv, signing_input); signing_input = 64*0x20 || context || 0x00 || transcript_hash. The expected signatures are produced with crypto/ed25519 directly, NOT SignCertVerify/certVerifySigningInput — independent of the implementation. The Ed25519 primitive is anchored to RFC 8032 §7.1 TEST 1/TEST 2 (the consuming test reproduces the published pubkeys + signatures first). Ed25519 is deterministic (RFC 8032), so any conforming signer reproduces these. transcript_hash inputs are the Transcript KAT's TH_sId (server) / TH_cId (client) — the points each role signs per spec §6.1. Keys are the RFC 8032 test keypairs (server=TEST1, client=TEST2).

rfc8032_ed25519.description: RFC 8032 §7.1 Ed25519 test vectors; anchor the Ed25519 primitive (pubkey derivation + deterministic signature).

spec: N-PAMP draft-00 handshake binding (spec/10) §6.1 (CertVerify); RFC 8446 §4.4.3; Ed25519 RFC 8032
